title: FP8 (W8A8 dynamic) vs AWQ-int4  (+ draft = production spec-decode)
subtitle: 2x R9700 TP=2, single-user, fp8 KV. Decode bars = short-ctx no-spec (2026-05-27 ref); max-context + spec RE-MEASURED 2026-05-31 post-iommu=pt
note: iommu=pt cleared a TP2 long-context P2P wedge (NCCL renegotiation storm; no-spec decode collapse 0.68->16.83 tok/s @131K). KEY 256K finding (re-measured 2026-05-31; full detail in benchmarks/fp8-256k-campaign-2026-05-31.json): in FP8 only the two A3B-MoE models reach TRUE single-user 256K (no-spec). FP8 + draft @ 256K works ONLY for Coder-30B (EAGLE3 — tiny 361MB draft, pure MoE) -> ~86 tok/s coherent, 256K served. Qwen3.6-35B DFlash gives 57.5 tok/s SHORT-ctx but OOMs at 256K in FP8 (BF16 draft + DeltaNet/fused-MoE prefill) -> AWQ-int4 is the 256K-spec path (80). Dense/SWA cap below 256K in FP8: Devstral-2-24B ~131K single-seq @mem0.90 (BF16 vision tower eats KV; pool 180K), gemma-4-26B ~32-64K (torch_native attention has NO FlashAttention -> global-attn layers OOM at long prefill; Triton attn crashes with SWA on RDNA4). Decode bars (fp8_toks/awq_toks) are the short-ctx 2026-05-27 snapshot, NOT re-measured; the re-measured no-spec @32K post-fix is ~18/18 for Coder/Qwen3.6-35B, ~23 Devstral-2, ~9.9 gemma. DeltaNet/VL/gemma-31 rows retain 2026-05-27 values pending re-measure.
